outcomes.consensus_answer: Maybe, but the effect is unclear. The cross-sectional study reported no cases of AIS in the HPV vaccine group; one pre-post vaccine introduction study reported an increase in AIS incidence, while the other reported a reduction.

outcomes.consensus_answer: It is very uncertain whether organised trauma systems reduce mortality or improve survival. One study reported reduced mortality with a relative effect of −30%; change in level −2.02 (95% CI: −3.17 to −0.86); change in slope −0.10 (95% CI: −0.53 to 0.33). A second study reported improved survival with a relative effect of 14%; change in level 0.13 (95% CI: −0.50 to 0.76); change in slope 0.08 (95% CI: 0.01 to 0.15).

outcomes.question: Does an organised trauma system reduce mortality or improve survival in injured patients relative to usual care at 6 to 9 years of follow-up?

outcomes.consensus_answer: It is very uncertain whether designated trauma centres reduce mortality. For all patients, the relative effect was −42%; change in level −3.60 (95% CI: −11.26 to 4.07); change in slope −0.51 (95% CI: −2.26 to 1.23). For participants with Injury Severity Score 15–24, the relative effect was −44%; change in level −8.80 (95% CI: −29.61 to 12.00); change in slope −0.96 (95% CI: −5.69 to 3.77). For participants with Injury Severity Score > 24, the relative effect was −55%; change in level −17.87 (95% CI: −47.12 to 11.37); change in slope −1.90 (95% CI: −8.55 to 4.75). For paediatric participants, the relative effect was −84%; change in level −18.56 (95% CI: −30.11 to −7.01); change in slope −1.12 (95% CI: −3.04 to 0.80). Mortality in adults was also assessed at 6 years’ follow-up: relative effect −67%; change in level −17.52 (95% CI: −42.27 to 7.23); change in slope −2.09 (95% CI: −6.22 to 2.04).

outcomes.question: Does treatment in a designated trauma centre reduce mortality in injured patients relative to usual care at 6 to 11 years of follow-up?

outcomes.consensus_answer: Uncertain; craniotomy with haematoma evacuation may increase good functional outcome, but the evidence is very uncertain. The relative risk is 1.41 (95% CI: 0.77 to 2.55).
